task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Initiate the connection to the Tor network
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Connect' button is clicked on the Tor Browser startup screen, or a connection progress bar is visible.

task_completion.description: Successfully connect to the Tor network
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser default homepage (e.g., 'Explore. Privately.') or a successfully loaded webpage is visible, indicating the connection process has completed.
The Security Level popup (accessed via the shield icon in the toolbar) or the Privacy & Security section in the browser settings (about:preferences) is open.

task_completion.description: Change the security level to 'Safer' or 'Safest'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The radio button or selection for either 'Safer' or 'Safest' is visually checked/highlighted in the Security Level settings, or the preference 'browser.security_level.security_slider' is set to 2 (Safer) or 1 (Safest) in about:config.

integrity.description: Agent used the Tor Browser interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the Tor Browser GUI (or about:config) rather than a terminal window editing background configuration files like prefs.js.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Tor Browser
integrity.visual_evidence: The application shown is the genuine Tor Browser with its characteristic UI elements (e.g., onion menu, shield icon), not a mock-up or different browser.

integrity.description: Agent did not tamper with evaluation scripts
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent opening, editing, or manipulating bash scripts (like export_result.sh or setup_task.sh) in a text editor or terminal to fake success.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial security level is 'standard'. The expected final security level is either 'safer' or 'safest'. If the agent uses the GUI, verify the radio button selection. If the agent uses 'about:config', the preference 'browser.security_level.security_slider' maps to 1 for 'safest', 2 for 'safer', and 4 for 'standard'. Ignore any bash script evaluation logic; rely entirely on the visual state of the browser.
